test_cases.input_summary: 'The Year After' — longitudinal audio documentary series tracking one person's first year after a major life event. 12 × 25-minute episodes, 10pm slot. Small indie production company pitching. No pilot. No presenter attachment.
test_cases.output_summary: Full pitch with 6 sections. Editorial proposition is clear and specific, avoids buzzwords. Format section visualises an episode from start to finish including the editorial shift at Episode 4-5. Why Now correctly identifies the serialised audio market gap. Production credentials section flagged as placeholder. Series proposal offers commissioning flexibility.
test_cases.notes: Editorial proposition paragraph is the strongest element — the 'one promise' framing is genuinely commissioning-ready. Relevance slightly marked: the audience section included a claim about 10pm slot research that was not in the input — defensible journalistically but the skill should not present unverified claims as evidence. Actionability: document used in a commissioning meeting with minor redaction of production credentials placeholder.

test_cases.input_summary: Weekly science explainer magazine show, 30 minutes, daytime. Target: public radio network. Comparable: Radio 4-style accessible science. Presenter attached (science journalist with podcast following). 6-episode pilot run proposed.
test_cases.output_summary: Pitch correctly uses the attached presenter's podcast following as evidence of audience demand. Format section maps each episode's structure clearly. Why Now points to science literacy gap rather than making a general 'science is popular' claim. Series proposal correctly frames the pilot-first approach.
test_cases.notes: Strong relevance — all input information is used purposefully. Consistency: no invented facts. Coherence: the audience section moved between two audience descriptions (science-curious general listener and science-adjacent professional) without fully resolving which is the primary target. One paragraph would resolve this.

test_cases.input_summary: True crime audio documentary series, 6 × 40-minute episodes, targeting a commercial network evening slot. Case involves a cold case wrongful conviction. Independent producer with one previous commissioned series. Taster tape available.
test_cases.output_summary: Pitch correctly front-loads the editorial proposition (wrongful conviction + cold case = both crime appeal and justice journalism) without sensationalising. Why Now references the regulatory environment around true crime responsibly. Series proposal includes the taster tape as a commissioning accelerant. Production credentials section uses the one previous commission effectively.
test_cases.notes: The dual editorial proposition (crime + justice journalism) is well-handled. Professionalism: the document correctly avoids sensationalist language and frames the ethical handling of the subject as a production value. Consistency: one sentence implied the wrongful conviction was confirmed — the input described it as alleged; this required correction before submission.
